title: London

jokes.text: I believe she's referring to me, son.
jokes.setup: Diane asks to talk to Ron in private, her father assumes she means him
jokes.punchline: The formal, obvious correction delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson delivery - formal and matter-of-fact about something obvious

jokes.text: Standard birth control methods aren't usually effective against a Swanson.
jokes.setup: Diane mentions they were careful with birth control
jokes.punchline: Ron treats his virility like a medical condition that transcends normal contraception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ron's masculine pride expressed through absurd medical terminology

jokes.text: Most likely in a canoe. I actually built a canoe for that purpose.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions taking Diane somewhere romantic, perhaps in a canoe
jokes.punchline: The escalation from 'perhaps' to 'most likely' to having already built the canoe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character beat - the practical woodworker who builds solutions before admitting to plans

jokes.text: Except a destination wedding.
jokes.setup: Ron says he'll give her anything in the world
jokes.punchline: Her one condition is specifically about NOT doing something elaborate
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of the romantic gesture - she wants less, not more

jokes.text: we could go up to the fourth floor right now.
jokes.setup: Discussing how simple they want their wedding to be
jokes.punchline: Ron's idea of simple is literally going upstairs in the government building
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Ron - practical to the point of absurdity about romance

jokes.text: There's nothing I love more than squeegeeing slime off a highway underpass.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining where they've been
jokes.punchline: Her genuine enthusiasm for the most unglamorous civic duty
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - finding joy in the mundane aspects of government work

jokes.text: Are you dead? You look like you're in rictus.
jokes.setup: Leslie is frozen with shock at the wedding news
jokes.punchline: Using the medical term 'rictus' to describe Leslie's expression
jokes.explanation: Good use of vocabulary for comedy - rictus is perfectly descriptive and clinical

jokes.text: and I do not have time to talk about this.
jokes.setup: Leslie expressing overwhelming joy about Ron's wedding
jokes.punchline: Immediately cutting off her own emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Leslie's efficiency even overriding her emotional moments

jokes.text: This is like a waking nightmare of happiness.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to process the rushed wedding
jokes.punchline: The oxymoron of a 'nightmare of happiness' perfectly captures her overwhelm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction that perfectly captures Leslie's overwhelming joy and need for control

jokes.text: Why don't you hold this colorful bouquet of highlighters?
jokes.setup: Leslie frantically trying to create wedding elements
jokes.punchline: Office supplies as wedding bouquet
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing Leslie's resourcefulness and government setting

jokes.text: Why is there no rice in this municipal government building?
jokes.setup: Leslie frantically trying to make the wedding proper
jokes.punchline: Expecting a government building to stock wedding supplies
jokes.explanation: Leslie's unrealistic expectations when trying to create perfection instantly

jokes.text: Haven't I done a quickie wedding for you before?
jokes.setup: Ron asking for an immediate wedding
jokes.punchline: The justice of peace recognizing Ron from previous rushed marriages
jokes.explanation: References Ron's history with the Tammys without being explicit

jokes.text: Diane Tammy Lewis.
jokes.setup: Giving her full name for the license
jokes.punchline: Using 'Tammy' as her middle name, invoking Ron's ex-wife trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake-out that plays on Ron's established Tammy phobia

jokes.text: Just kidding. It's Elizabeth.
jokes.setup: The Tammy reveal causing panic
jokes.punchline: Immediate relief with the real, harmless name
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to the Tammy fake-out, though the setup does most of the work

jokes.text: Webster's Dictionary defines 'friend'...
jokes.setup: Leslie getting 10 seconds for a short speech
jokes.punchline: Starting with the most cliché speech opening possible
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Leslie's inability to be brief even with constraints

jokes.text: The reception will be held in each of our individual houses, alone.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony concluding
jokes.punchline: Ron's anti-social approach to celebrating extends to his own reception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - even his wedding reception must be solitary

jokes.text: Pretend this is rice.
jokes.setup: Leslie throwing something at the newlyweds
jokes.punchline: Having to use substitute materials for the rice-throwing tradition
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Leslie's rice quest, though not particularly strong

jokes.text: Last week, I was an answer in the Pawnee Journal's crossword puzzle. The clue? 'Who's the worst?'
jokes.setup: Leslie describing the attacks against her
jokes.punchline: Being reduced to a crossword answer with a brutal clue
jokes.explanation: Creative way to show public disapproval through a specific, humiliating example

jokes.text: But not too happy.
jokes.setup: Impossible demands about slug removal: remove them but don't kill them, make them happy
jokes.punchline: Adding another contradictory requirement
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist escalation but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Ann, you cunning, pliable, chestnut-haired sunfish
jokes.setup: Leslie quoting herself to Ann
jokes.punchline: The completely random and overly specific compliment-insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie random compliment that somehow works as both endearing and bizarre

jokes.text: Life is precious and every day is a miracle.
jokes.setup: Ann describing their mundane slug problem discussion
jokes.punchline: Chris finding profound meaning in absolutely everything
jokes.explanation: Typical Chris optimism but not particularly clever execution

jokes.text: What a freak.
jokes.setup: Chris's over-the-top positive reaction to mundane problems
jokes.punchline: Ann's blunt assessment of her boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ann's exasperation with Chris's intensity

jokes.text: You see, you are Hydrox, Mr. Haverford. We are Oreo.
jokes.setup: Tom confused about why someone would copy his store exactly
jokes.punchline: Using the cookie comparison to explain who's the original vs the successful copy
jokes.explanation: Clever business metaphor that's both accurate and insulting

jokes.text: My client is not Jason Statham.
jokes.setup: Tom wondering about the mysterious investor's transportation abilities
jokes.punchline: Lawyer preemptively denying a completely random celebrity
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity denial is mildly funny but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's not LeBron James.
jokes.setup: Tom guessing about NBA trophies storage
jokes.punchline: Another preemptive celebrity denial
jokes.explanation: Running gag but diminishing returns on the same joke structure

jokes.text: Oh, no, I just. I got a fly.
jokes.setup: Ben seeming to have a brilliant idea
jokes.punchline: Deflating the moment with a completely mundane explanation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expectation but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: I just assumed you were lying.
jokes.setup: April revealing Leslie actually won an award
jokes.punchline: Leslie's casual assumption that April was making it up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie knows April's tendencies

jokes.text: History began on July 4th, 1776. Everything before that was a mistake.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about exploring London's history
jokes.punchline: Ron's completely serious American exceptionalism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson - absurdly patriotic and dismissive of anything non-American

jokes.text: A steak dinner, a glass of Lagavulin whiskey, then vigorous lovemaking for two hours, and we're both asleep by 8:30.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his ideal honeymoon plan
jokes.punchline: The methodical, scheduled approach to romance ending with an early bedtime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment combining his love of routine with unexpected TMI

jokes.text: Bong. Bong. Bong.
jokes.setup: Andy remembering a British kid who called him Big Ben
jokes.punchline: Recreating the clock sound while realizing the kid might have hated him
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy obliviousness to being bullied, with physical comedy

jokes.text: After all, we did come all the way down under.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to rationalize doing a British accent
jokes.punchline: Confusing England with Australia
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy geographical confusion, though relatively predictable

jokes.text: Your Royal Excellency, Lord Edgar Darby Covington, Fourteenth Earl of Cornwall upon Thames, Twenty-ninth Baron of Hartfordshire.
jokes.setup: Teaching proper address protocols
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously long and elaborate formal title
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the fake-out but the length makes it more tedious than funny

jokes.text: Look. A clock. We don't have that in America.
jokes.setup: Tourism in London
jokes.punchline: Ron's deliberately obtuse dismissal of Big Ben
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron anti-Europe sentiment expressed through willful ignorance

jokes.text: Try the Sears Tower, friend.
jokes.setup: Comparing London architecture
jokes.punchline: Dismissing British landmarks in favor of Chicago architecture
jokes.explanation: Continues Ron's dismissive American superiority theme

jokes.text: Of course you do. That's the most wonderful piece of paper in the world. Accept it.
jokes.setup: Being told they don't accept American currency
jokes.punchline: Ron's insistence that American money should be universally accepted
jokes.explanation: Ron's patriotic arrogance extending to currency exchange

jokes.text: Enjoy the fact that your royal overlords are a frail old woman and a tiny baby.
jokes.setup: Being frustrated with British customs
jokes.punchline: Ron's dismissive description of the British royal family
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly insulting while technically accurate description of the monarchy

jokes.text: Ann and Chris? What are they doing here?
jokes.setup: Tom following the address to confront his business rival
jokes.punchline: Finding his friends at what should be the rival's location
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the reveal but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Then, you stole my daughter's virginity.
jokes.setup: Dr. Saperstein explaining why he's targeting Tom
jokes.punchline: The awkward, personal revelation in a medical setting
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but effective escalation of the father's grievances

jokes.text: I had to buy her a new one so she could drive to divinity school.
jokes.setup: Describing daughter's damaged BMW
jokes.punchline: The contrast between luxury car and religious education
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between materialism and spirituality

jokes.text: And Tom and his weird feud with our doctor.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment with the heartbeat
jokes.punchline: The intrusion of Tom's bizarre situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan acknowledgment of the absurd situation interrupting their moment

jokes.text: He called it. That's the rule.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming the blue helicopter
jokes.punchline: A Lord respecting playground rules over social protocol
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of expected aristocratic behavior

jokes.text: Oh, no, actually, we do own that.
jokes.setup: Listing properties his family owns
jokes.punchline: Correcting himself to include even more wealth
jokes.explanation: Shows the casual, overwhelming wealth in an understated way

jokes.text: Where do you think you are, the moon?
jokes.setup: Andy asking if they have ice cream in England
jokes.punchline: Eddie's incredulous response about basic civilization
jokes.explanation: Good response to Andy's oblivious question about basic amenities

jokes.text: and instead, just walk up to the mic and meow really loudly for eight minutes.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for feedback on her speech
jokes.punchline: April's completely unhelpful, chaotic alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect April suggestion - destructive, random, and specifically timed

jokes.text: She's gallivanting around Europe, like some kind of European.
jokes.setup: Attacking Leslie for being in Europe
jokes.punchline: The tautological accusation that being in Europe makes you European
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic that perfectly captures political attack rhetoric

jokes.text: 35% said they were confused by the way the question was posed.
jokes.setup: Perd conducting a confusing poll
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his own confusing question structure
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Perd's consistently confusing journalistic style

jokes.text: but I told him that you made me sign up for a bunch of credit cards, and then maxed them out by buying illegal white jaguar fur coats.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining his lies about Tom
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and ridiculous fabrications
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity that fits Jean-Ralphio's tendency to embellish

jokes.text: They filled it with broccoli just to add insult to injury.
jokes.setup: Describing the piñata made of her face
jokes.punchline: The specific choice of healthy vegetables instead of candy as extra cruelty
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that makes the public humiliation both more specific and more cruel

jokes.text: There was a statue of a lion inside, and I found a yo-yo.
jokes.setup: Describing being lost in his family's hedge maze
jokes.punchline: The random treasures found during his adventure
jokes.explanation: Whimsical details that highlight the absurd scale of wealth

jokes.text: I meant Andy. I want Andy to stay.
jokes.setup: Ben assuming the job offer is for him
jokes.punchline: Eddie preferring Andy despite Ben's obvious qualifications
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts expectations about who would be valuable

jokes.text: I meant Andy. I want Andy to stay.
jokes.setup: Ben assuming the lord wants him to stay and help
jokes.punchline: Lord Covington preferring Andy's childlike enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character insight - choosing Andy over the qualified candidate

jokes.text: and the reindeer was delicious.
jokes.setup: Describing a beautiful birthday celebration with a dancing reindeer
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of eating the performer
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark turn that completely subverts the wholesome setup

jokes.text: Pawnee is filled with a bunch of peepee-heads.
jokes.setup: Leslie's mounting frustration with her hometown
jokes.punchline: The childish insult delivered in a formal international setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Leslie's breakdown - childish but memorable

jokes.text: Pawnee is filled with a bunch of peepee-heads.
jokes.setup: Leslie's international speech about her hometown
jokes.punchline: Using childish insult in formal international setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie breakdown - maintaining politeness even while insulting her constituents

jokes.text: When did you make the switch to boxer briefs?
jokes.setup: Donna demonstrating her observational skills
jokes.punchline: Making Tom uncomfortable with intimate details
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Donna's observational skills with uncomfortable specificity

jokes.text: Give it to me. I want milk.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing babies rooting for milk
jokes.punchline: Jerry's disturbing baby voice impression
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably specific Jerry moment but not particularly clever

jokes.text: One bearded lady. She got all rude when I marveled at her.
jokes.setup: Andy describing Piccadilly Circus disappointment
jokes.punchline: His oblivious offense at someone's reaction to being 'marveled' at
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy obliviousness to social inappropriateness

jokes.text: You shined shoes for over two years and never made a profit.
jokes.setup: Andy worried about running a non-profit
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact point about his business track record
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reframes Andy's failure as qualification

jokes.text: Right now it looks like I'm talking to a giant crab.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her jet lag and medication mix
jokes.punchline: Hallucinating while trying to reassure friends
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her trying to function while clearly impaired

jokes.text: I have done nothing wrong. Ever, in my life.
jokes.setup: Confrontation about lying to his father
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's completely delusional self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio delusion delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Tell me you love us, and then, admit this guy looks like Beaker from the Muppets.
jokes.setup: Tense family confrontation
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's inability to stay serious and random insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Jean-Ralphio chaos but the Beaker comparison isn't particularly strong

jokes.text: And me, Odie the dog, Garfield's enemy.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing herself to help with slugs
jokes.punchline: April's random cartoon character identification
jokes.explanation: Classic April randomness that's both unhelpful and specific

jokes.text: Because it's lower in sodium?
jokes.setup: Discussing baby names based on size comparisons
jokes.punchline: Chris's health-focused reasoning for name preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris response that applies his health fixation to baby naming

jokes.text: Sincerely, Satan.
jokes.setup: Beautiful, heartfelt letter about Leslie
jokes.punchline: April's signature completely undermining the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - genuine emotion hidden behind her dark persona. The contrast makes both the sentiment and the joke more powerful.

jokes.text: I don't know what she thought I'd get out of that.
jokes.setup: Ron reading a romantic poem by a Scottish lake
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to appreciate poetry or romance
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron missing the point of Leslie's romantic gesture entirely

jokes.text: Failed experiment. Take it off.
jokes.setup: Ben wearing a barrister's wig
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate rejection of her own romantic idea
jokes.explanation: Good end-of-episode physical comedy though not particularly memorable

tmdb_overview: Leslie wins an award from an international Women in Government organization, and travels to London with Ben, Andy, April and Ron to accept it.
insight: London trip launches Season 6 with 66 jokes at steady 1.27-per-minute pace.
